title: Necrotizing Enterocolitis
chief_complaint: Premature infant (32 weeks) in NICU. Developed feeding intolerance, abdominal distension, and bloody stools over 12 hours. Now lethargic and apneic.

physical_exam.General: Lethargic, hypotonic, mottled skin, capillary refill 5 seconds
physical_exam.Abdomen: Severely distended, tense, shiny. Erythema of abdominal wall. Absent bowel sounds. Tender to palpation. Palpable bowel loops (fixed, dilated loops).

hidden_data.abdominal_xr: Pneumatosis intestinalis (gas within bowel wall) in right lower quadrant. Portal venous gas. Fixed, dilated bowel loops. No free air currently.
hidden_data.cmp: Na 130, K+ 6.0, Lactate 8.5, HCO3 12 (severe metabolic acidosis)

correct_diagnosis: Necrotizing Enterocolitis (NEC) - Bell Stage IIB (Definite NEC with Pneumatosis and Portal Venous Gas)
key_learning_points: NEC = most common GI emergency in preterm neonates. Incidence inversely proportional to gestational age. Peak: 2-3 weeks of life
Pathophysiology: Intestinal ischemia + formula feeding + bacterial overgrowth → mucosal injury → gas-producing bacteria invade bowel wall (pneumatosis) → necrosis → perforation
Bell staging: IA (suspect: ileus, distension), IB (gross blood in stool), IIA (definite: pneumatosis), IIB (portal venous gas), IIIA (perforation), IIIB (peritonitis)

Treatment: NPO, NG decompression, IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics (Ampicillin + Gentamicin + Metronidazole x10-14 days). Serial abdominal XRs q6-8h. Surgical consult for perforation (free air) or clinical deterioration
Indications for surgery: Free air (perforation), Peritonitis, Fixed dilated loop persisting >24h, Clinical deterioration despite maximal medical therapy, Portal venous gas (relative)

feedback.attending_note: Immediate: NPO, NG to low intermittent suction, IV fluids (D10¼NS), Ampicillin 100mg/kg + Gentamicin 4mg/kg + Metronidazole 7.5mg/kg IV. Serial abdominal XRs q6-8h. Surgical consult NOW for portal venous gas. Transfuse platelets if <50K. Correct acidosis. Intubate for apnea. Family counseling - mortality 20-30%.
